title: andropogon glomeratus subsp. reinoldii
intro: Andropogon glomeratus subsp. reinoldii, also known as Big Bluestem, is a perennial grass native to the Americas, specifically found in the prairies and grasslands of the United States and Canada. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene and other compounds. The health benefits of Big Bluestem include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Big Bluestem is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be used as a poultice or applied topically to wounds and skin irritations.
